skill.name: clinicaltrials-db
skill.description: Query the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 to search for clinical trials, retrieve detailed study protocols, and analyze recruitment status; use when you need to find trials by condition/drug, export results, or verify study details by NCT ID.
skill.summary: Natural language interface to the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for searching and retrieving clinical trial data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All high-severity static findings are false positives: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns appear in JSON audit metadata and markdown documentation text, not in actual cryptographic code. All 19 'shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting characters, not executable commands. The only confirmed risk is legitimate network access to the public ClinicalTrials.gov API. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was identified. The skill is safe to publish with a low risk advisory for network access.

low_findings.title: HTTP Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The Python script uses requests.get() to query the ClinicalTrials.gov public API. This is the documented and legitimate purpose of the skill. All requests target the official clinicaltrials.gov domain. No authentication credentials are transmitted.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Reference documentation and SKILL.md contain hardcoded URLs pointing to clinicaltrials.gov and the skill's GitHub repository. These are informational references, not executable endpoints, and point to legitimate public resources.

content.user_title: Search and Analyze Clinical Trials with Natural Language
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually searching ClinicalTrials.gov for relevant studies. This skill lets you search, filter, and retrieve detailed clinical trial data using simple natural language queries through Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Search clinical trials by condition, intervention, and recruitment status using natural language
Retrieve complete study protocols including eligibility criteria and phase information
Look up studies by NCT identifier for verified and authoritative results
Export trial metadata in JSON format for downstream analysis or reporting
Filter results by recruitment status such as recruiting, completed, or terminated
limitations: Subject to ClinicalTrials.gov API rate limits of approximately 50 requests per minute
Retrieves protocol-level data only; does not include study results or publication links
Requires Python 3.9 and the requests library for running the included scripts
Cannot perform statistical analysis or interpret clinical trial data

use_cases.title: Find Recruiting Cancer Trials for Patient Referral
use_cases.description: Search for actively recruiting cancer studies by condition type to identify potential trials for patient referral and enrollment options.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical research coordinators and healthcare providers

use_cases.title: Verify Study Status Before Systematic Review
use_cases.description: Retrieve official study status and protocol details by NCT ID to ensure data accuracy before including studies in a meta-analysis or systematic review.

use_cases.title: Monitor Drug Development Pipeline
use_cases.description: Search for new interventional studies involving specific drugs or biologics to track competitor clinical development activity and emerging therapies.
use_cases.target_user: Pharmaceutical industry analysts and competitive intelligence teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Trial Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find clinical trials for [condition, e.g., type 2 diabetes] that are currently recruiting.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup for available trials in a disease area

prompt_templates.title: Intervention-Specific Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for clinical trials studying [drug name, e.g., semaglutide] and show me the study phase and sponsor for each result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Investigating trials for a specific treatment or drug

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Protocol Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the full protocol for NCT[NCT ID NUMBER] including the eligibility criteria, exclusion criteria, study phase, and locations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Verifying study details for a known clinical trial registration

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Trial Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recruiting phase 3 trials for both [drug A] and [drug B], then compare their enrollment counts and primary outcome measures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing competing interventions in the same therapeutic area

output_examples.input: Find recruiting clinical trials for lung cancer.
output_examples.output: Ten recruiting lung cancer studies were found. Each result includes the NCT identifier, brief study title, sponsor name, study phase, and recruitment status. Results are sorted by relevance per the ClinicalTrials.gov API.
Full protocol details retrieved including study title, sponsor organization, study phase, enrollment target, eligibility criteria, inclusion and exclusion criteria, and study locations.

output_examples.input: Search for trials studying aspirin for cardiovascular prevention.
output_examples.output: A list of interventional studies involving aspirin for cardiovascular conditions, showing study status (recruiting, active, completed), phase, sponsor type, and brief summaries for each match.

best_practices: Start with broad search terms and narrow down using filters for more targeted results.
Always verify critical study information by retrieving full protocol details using the NCT ID.
Cache search results locally when performing repeated analysis to reduce API calls and stay within rate limits.
anti_patterns: Using this skill to diagnose medical conditions or recommend specific treatments based on trial protocol data.
Making rapid repeated API calls without respecting the rate limit of 50 requests per minute.
Interpreting trial recruitment status as definitive evidence of drug safety or efficacy without consulting full study results.

faq.question: What is ClinicalTrials.gov?
faq.answer: ClinicalTrials.gov is a database of publicly and privately funded clinical studies conducted around the world, maintained by the U.S. National Library of Medicine.

faq.question: What data can this skill retrieve?
faq.answer: It can search for studies by condition or intervention and retrieve protocol-level data including eligibility criteria, study phase, sponsor, and recruitment status.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: Yes. ClinicalTrials.gov provides a free public API, and this skill uses that API without any paid tiers or authentication requirements.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for regulatory submissions?
faq.answer: The skill retrieves data from the official ClinicalTrials.gov API, but always verify critical data directly on ClinicalTrials.gov for regulatory or clinical decision-making.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 is publicly accessible without authentication or API keys.

faq.question: What programming knowledge is needed?
faq.answer: Basic familiarity with Python is helpful for running the included scripts, but the skill can be used through natural language commands via Claude, Codex, or Claude Code without programming.
